relevant_prior_traps: Source and built SDK subpaths can split a canonical tag identity unless every entry resolves through one module.
A successful remote effect with a lost response requires exhaustive paginated marker recovery before retrying the write.
Database publication fences must not remain held across unbounded remote calls.
A file-wide binding scan is not a substitute for lexical scope resolution.
avoidances_that_worked: Declare effects through controllers, resources, atoms, or ordinary required tags and substitute them at createScope.
Join in-flight resource resolution before structured close completes.
Use one deadline across the entire remote publication interval held under the database fence.
Require deterministic replay plus a separate reviewer that can reject the candidate.
stale_or_rejected_learnings: Do not claim a callback speedup or full performance no-regression result from the current benchmark evidence.
Do not treat the managed package-export child-process harness as executed; direct built ESM and CJS identity checks are the available evidence.
Do not add a tag taxonomy, spawn primitive, worker pool primitive, or MCP surface without a concrete use case and fresh DKR.

suggested_dkr_probes.probe: Re-run package export identity in an environment that permits the npm child process.
suggested_dkr_probes.budget: one focused package build and harness run
suggested_dkr_probes.steering_decision: Only needed when the package-export surface is touched or before release.

suggested_dkr_probes.probe: Design a stable paired benchmark for callback ergonomics with variance control.
suggested_dkr_probes.steering_decision: Only needed before making a performance claim.

context_fit: Apply only when changing the pumped-fn Lite lifecycle, SDK session or agent graph, provider adapters, or the issue-triage production example.
recertification: Re-read every standing metric on the next touch; direct production and independence evidence expire immediately when affected code changes.
